body:
Bill -

Welcome aboard. Let's get the regulatory matters sorted out so we know where things land.

I'm assuming the following three EES matters are in flight:

1. The FERC rate case matter (I believe this involves the EES retail contract tariff issues)
2. The compliance filing matter (state regulatory)
3. The contract interpretation dispute (need to confirm which forum)

Please confirm if that's accurate. Here's my initial read on ownership:

- Items 1 and 2 should travel with you to ETS. The rate case and compliance work will need ETS coordination given the pipeline interface, and frankly we need that expertise in-house.

- Item 3 - the contract dispute - I'm less certain this should come to ETS. If it's primarily an EES retail contract interpretation issue without direct pipeline implications, it may make more sense for EES to retain. That said, I want to make sure we don't create a gap.

Can you pull together a one-pager on each matter by EOD Thursday? I'd like to get a clear handoff plan to you and to whoever stays behind at EES by end of next week.

I'll loop in [EES General Counsel] to confirm this approach - want to make sure we're aligned before we finalize anything.

df

Drew -

Confirmed on the three matters. Your read is accurate.

On ownership, I'd generally agree with your assessment:

Items 1 and 2 - agreed, these should come to ETS. The rate case and compliance filing both have direct pipeline interface issues that will need ETS coordination.

Item 3 - the contract interpretation dispute involves the EES retail contract with [counterparty]. My understanding is the pipeline is not a named party, but the dispute touches on whether the retail contract's "pipeline access" provisions are enforceable against TW. That said, EES is the contracting entity and holds the client relationship. I'd recommend EES retain primary ownership with ETS available for consultation if the TW provisions are implicated.

One-pagers by EOD Thursday is workable. I'll coordinate with [EES counsel] on the handoff materials so we can send a consolidated plan next week.

I'll flag if any of these timelines create issues - let me know.

Bill

That works for me. Agreed on all three.

Item 3 - your recommendation makes sense. EES retains primary, ETS on standby for consultation if the TW provisions get implicated. Let's document that clearly so there's no ambiguity on roles.

On coordination: I'll reach out to [EES GC] directly to get alignment on the overall approach before we finalize. You handle the one-pagers and coordinate with [EES counsel] on the handoff package - that way we can send a consolidated plan to both sides next week.

One thing to flag: if the TW provisions do become an issue in item 3, I want to know about it early. Don't wait for it to become a problem - loop me in as soon as there's any indication the pipeline side gets drawn in.

We're good here. Thanks for moving quickly on this.

df

Understood. I'll get the one-pagers to you by EOD Thursday and coordinate with [EES counsel] on the handoff package. 

On the escalation protocol for Item 3 - will do. I'll flag promptly if the TW provisions come into play.

We're aligned. Plan is set - one-pagers Thursday, consolidated handoff next week. I'll touch base with [EES GC] this week. We're good to move forward.

df
